test_cases.title: RTL generation produces all structural elements for a complex design
preconditions: A project exists with a complete, fully-connected clock tree design
All nodes have required properties set
test_cases.given: Project 'p1' contains: PLL1(output_freq=100MHz), PLL2(output_freq=200MHz), PLL3(output_freq=400MHz); Divider1(ratio=4, input from PLL1), Divider2(ratio=8, input from PLL2); Mux1(select_index=0, inputs from Divider1 and Divider2); ClockGate1(input from PLL3), ClockGate2(input from Mux1); ClockDomain1(input from ClockGate2); IP1(input from ClockGate1), IP2(input from ClockDomain1), IP3(input from ClockDomain1). All nodes connected.
test_cases.then: HTTP 200 response body field 'rtl' contains a string with: (a) 'module' and 'endmodule' keywords, (b) three clock input port declarations (one per PLL), (c) two 'always' blocks (one per Divider), (d) one 'assign' statement with a ternary operator (for Mux1 sel) and a module input port declaration for the Mux1 sel signal, (e) two AND gate expressions for ClockGate1 and ClockGate2 with module input port declarations for each Clock Gate's en signal, (f) a comment containing 'ClockDomain1' for the domain boundary, (g) three output assignments for IP1, IP2, IP3

test_cases.title: SDC generation produces create_clock and set_false_path for CDC design
preconditions: A project exists with two PLLs at different frequencies and a CDC crossing between them
test_cases.given: Project 'p2' contains: PLL1(output_freq=100MHz), PLL2(output_freq=200MHz); Divider1(ratio=2, input from PLL1); Mux1(select_index=0, inputs from Divider1 and PLL2); ClockDomainA(input from Mux1), ClockDomainB(input from PLL2); IP1(input from ClockDomainA), IP2(input from ClockDomainB); a direct wire from a node in ClockDomainA to a node in ClockDomainB creating a CDC crossing.
test_cases.then: HTTP 200 response body field 'sdc' contains a string with: (a) exactly two 'create_clock' commands, one referencing '100' MHz and one referencing '200' MHz, (b) at least one 'create_generated_clock' command with a '-source' argument for Divider1 and Mux1 outputs, (c) at least one 'set_clock_groups' command grouping Clock Domains with different frequencies, (d) at least one 'set_false_path' command covering the CDC crossing path

test_cases.title: Code generation rejected with 422 when Divider input port is unconnected
preconditions: A project exists where at least one Divider node has no wire connected to its input port
test_cases.given: Project 'p3' contains: PLL1(output_freq=100MHz), Divider1(ratio=4) with no input connection, IP1(input from PLL1). Divider1's input port is unconnected.
test_cases.then: HTTP 422 response; response body contains a non-empty 'incomplete_items' array; the array includes an entry with 'node_id' equal to Divider1's ID, 'node_type' equal to 'Divider', and a non-empty 'reason' string; no 'rtl' or 'sdc' fields are present in the response

test_cases.title: Code generation rejected with 422 when Mux select_index is out of range
preconditions: A project exists with a Mux whose select_index exceeds the number of connected inputs
test_cases.given: Project 'p4' contains: PLL1(output_freq=100MHz), PLL2(output_freq=200MHz), Mux1(select_index=5, inputs=[PLL1, PLL2], total 2 inputs), IP1(input from Mux1). Mux1.select_index=5 is out of range [0,1].
test_cases.then: HTTP 422 response; response body contains a non-empty 'incomplete_items' array; the array includes an entry with 'node_id' equal to Mux1's ID, 'node_type' equal to 'Mux', and a 'reason' string mentioning 'select_index' or 'out of range'; no 'rtl' or 'sdc' fields are present

test_cases.title: Code generation succeeds for a fully complete and connected design
preconditions: A project exists with all nodes fully connected and all required properties set
test_cases.given: Project 'p5' contains: PLL1(output_freq=50MHz), Divider1(ratio=2, input from PLL1), IP1(input from Divider1). All ports connected, all properties valid.
test_cases.then: HTTP 200 response; response body contains both 'rtl' and 'sdc' string fields with non-empty content; no 'incomplete_items' field or empty 'incomplete_items' array

test_cases.title: Preview endpoint returns both rtl and sdc string fields
preconditions: A project exists with at least one PLL and one IP Block fully connected
test_cases.given: Project 'p6' contains: PLL1(output_freq=200MHz), IP1(input from PLL1). All nodes connected.
test_cases.then: HTTP 200 response; response body is JSON with a top-level 'rtl' field of type string and a top-level 'sdc' field of type string; neither field is null or an empty string; no file is saved to disk as a side effect

test_cases.title: Download endpoint returns a ZIP archive containing .v and .sdc files
preconditions: A project named 'my_clock_design' exists with at least one PLL and one IP Block fully connected
test_cases.given: Project 'p7' with name 'my_clock_design' contains: PLL1(output_freq=100MHz), IP1(input from PLL1). All nodes connected.
test_cases.then: HTTP 200 response; response header 'Content-Type' is 'application/zip'; response body is a valid ZIP archive containing exactly two entries: 'my_clock_design.v' and 'my_clock_design.sdc'; both entries are non-empty

test_cases.title: Export produces a valid JSON document with all nodes and edges
preconditions: A project exists with 2 nodes and 1 edge
test_cases.given: Project 'p8' contains: PLL1(output_freq=300MHz), IP1(input from PLL1); one edge from PLL1 output port to IP1 input port.
test_cases.then: HTTP 200 response; response body is valid JSON with: (a) a top-level 'schema_version' field equal to '1.0', (b) a 'nodes' array of length 2 where each entry has 'id', 'type', 'properties', and 'position' fields, (c) an 'edges' array of length 1 where the entry has 'source' and 'target' port references; response header includes a content-disposition or content-type suggesting a file download

test_cases.title: Import of a valid JSON design creates a new project and returns 201
preconditions: A valid exported JSON design file is available with schema_version '1.0'
test_cases.given: A JSON payload with schema_version='1.0', nodes=[{id:'n1', type:'PLL', properties:{output_freq:100}, position:{x:0,y:0}}, {id:'n2', type:'IPBlock', properties:{name:'IP1',power_mw:5}, position:{x:200,y:0}}], edges=[{source:'n1:out', target:'n2:in'}].
test_cases.when: POST /api/projects/import is called with the JSON payload as the request body
test_cases.then: HTTP 201 response; response body contains a 'project_id' field with a non-null, non-empty string value; a subsequent GET /api/projects/{project_id} returns the imported nodes and edges

test_cases.title: Import rejected with 400 when schema_version is unsupported
preconditions: The system supports schema_version '1.0' only
test_cases.given: A JSON payload with schema_version='9.9', nodes=[], edges=[].
test_cases.when: POST /api/projects/import is called with the JSON payload
test_cases.then: HTTP 400 response; response body contains a non-empty 'errors' array; at least one error entry references 'schema_version' or 'unsupported version'

test_cases.title: Import rejected with 400 when required field 'nodes' is missing
preconditions: The import endpoint requires 'schema_version', 'nodes', and 'edges' as top-level fields
test_cases.given: A JSON payload with schema_version='1.0' and edges=[] but no 'nodes' field.
test_cases.when: POST /api/projects/import is called with the JSON payload
test_cases.then: HTTP 400 response; response body contains a non-empty 'errors' array; at least one error entry references the missing 'nodes' field

test_cases.title: Import rejected with 400 when a field has an invalid data type
preconditions: The import endpoint validates that 'nodes' must be an array and node 'type' must be a string
test_cases.given: A JSON payload with schema_version='1.0', nodes='not-an-array', edges=[].
test_cases.when: POST /api/projects/import is called with the JSON payload
test_cases.then: HTTP 400 response; response body contains a non-empty 'errors' array; at least one error entry references 'nodes' and a type mismatch or invalid type

test_cases.title: Generated Verilog passes IEEE 1364-2005 structural syntax checks
preconditions: The RTL generator function is available as a unit-testable module
A minimal valid design graph (1 PLL, 1 IP Block, 1 edge) is provided as input
test_cases.given: Design graph: PLL1(output_freq=100MHz) → IP1. Input passed directly to the RTL generator function.
test_cases.when: The RTL generator function is called with the design graph
test_cases.then: The returned string matches all of the following regex patterns: (a) /^module\s+\w+/m (module declaration), (b) /endmodule/m (module closure), (c) /\b(wire|reg)\b/m (valid signal declaration), (d) /;\s*$/m (semicolon-terminated statements); no regex pattern for 'syntax error' matches the output

test_cases.title: Generated SDC passes Synopsys Design Constraints syntax checks
preconditions: The SDC generator function is available as a unit-testable module
A design graph with 2 PLLs, 1 Divider, 1 Mux, and 1 CDC crossing is provided as input
test_cases.given: Design graph: PLL1(100MHz) → Divider1(ratio=2) → Mux1(sel=0); PLL2(200MHz) → Mux1; Mux1 → IP1; a CDC crossing from PLL1 domain to PLL2 domain. Passed to SDC generator function.
test_cases.when: The SDC generator function is called with the design graph
test_cases.then: The returned string matches all of the following regex patterns: (a) /^create_clock\s+-period\s+[\d.]+/m (create_clock with -period argument), (b) /^create_generated_clock\s+-source/m (create_generated_clock with -source argument), (c) /^set_clock_groups\s+/m (set_clock_groups command), (d) /^set_false_path\s+-from/m (set_false_path with -from argument); each command line ends with a valid Tcl line terminator (newline without backslash continuation on the last token)

test_cases.title: Code generation rejected with 422 for a project with zero nodes
preconditions: A project exists with no nodes and no edges
test_cases.given: Project 'p9' exists with an empty design: nodes=[], edges=[].
test_cases.then: HTTP 422 response; response body contains an 'error' or 'message' field with a non-empty string indicating the design is empty (e.g., contains 'empty' or 'no nodes'); no 'rtl' or 'sdc' fields are present in the response

test_cases.title: RTL download for zero-node project is rejected with 422
preconditions: A project exists with no nodes and no edges
test_cases.given: Project 'p10' exists with an empty design: nodes=[], edges=[].
test_cases.then: HTTP 422 response; response body contains an 'error' or 'message' field indicating the design is empty; no ZIP archive is returned; Content-Type is not 'application/zip'

test_cases.title: Download endpoint rejected with 422 for incomplete design (unconnected Divider)
preconditions: A project exists where at least one Divider node has no wire connected to its input port
test_cases.given: Project 'p11' contains: PLL1(output_freq=100MHz), Divider1(ratio=4) with no input connection, IP1(input from PLL1). Divider1's input port is unconnected.
test_cases.then: HTTP 422 response; response body contains a non-empty 'incomplete_items' array; the array includes an entry with 'node_id' equal to Divider1's ID, 'node_type' equal to 'Divider', and a non-empty 'reason' string; no ZIP archive is returned; Content-Type is not 'application/zip'

test_cases.title: Generated code includes generation timestamp and project name in comments
preconditions: The RTL and SDC generator functions are available as unit-testable modules
A minimal valid design graph (1 PLL, 1 IP Block, 1 edge) with project name 'TestProj' is provided as input
test_cases.given: Design graph: PLL1(output_freq=100MHz) → IP1, project name='TestProj'. Input passed directly to the RTL and SDC generator functions.
test_cases.when: The RTL generator and SDC generator functions are called with the design graph and project name
test_cases.then: The returned RTL string contains a comment line that includes the project name 'TestProj' and a generation timestamp in a recognizable date/time format; the returned SDC string also contains a comment line with the project name 'TestProj' and a generation timestamp
